name: Kaitlynn Anderson
outcome: I started as a March cohort and I am very serious about my studies. I am recently divorced and a survivor of domestic abuse and school has helped me immensely cope and make my life better for my children and myself. I have already been working on GitHub utilizing my own personal development through portfolios.

name: Dylan Snyder
outcome: Attending Maestro College for my Associate of Applied Science in Business Administration has been a truly transformative experience. From day one, the program's flexibility allowed me to take complete control of my education, giving me the freedom to balance my coursework with my professional life and learn at my own pace without feeling overwhelmed. What really sets Maestro College apart, though, is how the technology works with you. The AI-driven learning tools adapt seamlessly to my unique learning style, breaking down complex business theories and administration concepts in a way that feels tailormade for how I process information. It bridges the gap between challenging material and real-world application, making the entire educational journey feel intuitive and deeply supportive. Ultimately, this degree program has done so much more than just teach me the fundamentals of business administration—it has truly empowered me to take charge of my future. Having this structured yet adaptive education backing me up has given me the confidence and the concrete skills needed to unlock new professional opportunities and shape the career path I’ve always wanted. Maestro College doesn't just offer a degree; it provides the exact tools and flexibility required to turn personal goals into a tangible, successful reality.

name: Troy Moser
program: October 2025 Cohort - BS of Science - Business and AI Pathway
outcome: My website development beforehand was poor at best. What I have learned in my first year is amazing. I can now read, understand, and produce REAL code. Whether I'm Python, React, JavaScript, and Typescript. That's amazing to me! Not to mention it's really at my pace. Sometimes I'm a week behind because I'm struggling, other times I'm a week ahead because something clicked and I got it! I love that!

name: Precious Vicknair
outcome: Before enrolling in Maestro College I didn't know at all what Python, JavaScript, or Typescript were, just that they were computer words. I'm only in month 6 and now I can code at a decent proficiency, I can debug simple errors, and feel pretty good about the progress I'm making. I can make small, input/output programs in all three platforms.

name: Jay young
outcome:
I am an electrician by trade and decided I wanted to take it a step further .So I signed up for Maestros very first cohort. Right away , I began learning vital applicable skills that I could practice and apply immediately.Since then I have participated in several hackathons and have developed several open source agentic projects on solana blockchain. The pace and one-on-one experience offered at maestro enables me to learn at my own pace,and the curriculum is taught to me in the way I learn the best. I have since received a job offer from X-AI and am starting a brand new career. Thanks Maestro!

Full Submitted Proficiency: Ability to design and build complex agentic architecture using a variety of programming languages and frameworks.

name: Gage Hanback
outcome:
Aethel Cortex Vektor: Project Overview Aethel Cortex Vektor is a data engineering and orchestration engine built to automate B2B data and content workflows. Powered by the NexusPipeline framework, the platform replaces manual processes with a hands-off, self-sustaining pipeline. What It Does Automated Scraping & Engineering: Extracts raw, unstructured web data and cleans it into structured formats. Agentic AI Execution:Deploys autonomous AI agents to analyze the processed data and handle high-level tasks. Hands-Off SEO Pipelines: Automatically spins up optimized, context-driven content based on the engineered data inputs. The Stack & How It Was Built Python: The entire backend logic, data processing modules, and agent workflows are written in Python. GitHub Actions: Handles the CI/CD pipeline. Every update automatically triggers automated tests and builds to keep production stable. Package Distribution:Configured to compile the core framework and distribute it as a published Python package right through GitHub for quick deployment. Infrastructure: Hosted under a dedicated domain (aethelcortexvektors.com) with a lean, conversion-focused B2B landing page. The Bottom Line This tool bridges the gap between raw web data and production-ready output. By tying a modular Python backend directly to automated DevOps and AI agents, it eliminates the middleman entirely.

Full Submitted Proficiency: Made and deploying my very own saas tool coding this all on my own in python

name: Larry Hicks
outcome:
I designed an AI named Kaida. I used several avenues to build her, but maestro gave me the understanding of what I was doing. I developed a working relationship with two Maestro AIs that aided me in her development. Their guidance was unparalleled. Maestro taught me the I/OCE method, which included pseudocode. My development process grew refined due to this.

Full Submitted Proficiency: What haven't I learned? First, I learned the basics of writing code. Then, I learned functions and how to build a pipeline. I learned what 'main' does and how different functions should always have one job

name: Erica Holzman
outcome:
Maestro has given me the ability to branch out and expand my CPR business. I just went to MIT in Boston and taught CPR to high school students visting the campus for two weeks. I wouldn't have had the software to handle that without this class.

Full Submitted Proficiency: I own a CPR company this has helped me strengthe my skills in managing training sessions, maintaining compliance with certification standards

name: Sierra Makins
outcome:
Maestro has taught me how to publicly speak, how to be confident about my own work, how to organize and speak properly, how to do my work in a proper way where other people can read an interpret my work, as well as the ability to build projects that I have been wanting to build for years, but I didn’t know how to code or didn’t know the tools that were needed to do so. It’s even taught me newer subjects like psychology, and things about history. I didn’t know even (about) global warming. I came from Pottstown, Pennsylvania, and I’m sorry but my education system here is not the best, and this has opened up the world of education for me drastically. If this course shuts down, I don’t know what I would do, even transferring I would possibly have to be stuck with an in person school and have to start all over again.

Full Submitted Proficiency: I’ve learned how to do massive projects that I’ve been wanting to do for years, but didn’t know the coding to do so I struggle with autism ADHD in public speaking. This course has helped with it all.
